hero.name: Phan Minh Quân
hero.role: Final-year Student | Aspiring Cloud Security & Software Engineer
hero.tagline: Building automated security systems on AWS & robust applications
hero.introduction: I am a final-year student specializing in Network and Information Security at VKU. I have strong hands-on experience in Cloud Security, SIEM/SOAR systems, as well as full-stack mobile application development.

about.description: I am a final-year student at VKU focusing on Cloud Security, SIEM, and SOAR systems. I prefer hands-on learning by building real-world systems, ranging from Wazuh-based SIEM and AWS-integrated security pipelines to full-fledged mobile e-commerce applications. My goal is to leverage my software development background to become a highly effective Cloud Security Engineer in a professional environment.

projects.name: Load Balancing System using pfSense + HAProxy
projects.problem: Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance for a web system under increasing network traffic and potential ISP failures.
projects.description: Designed and implemented a full network load balancing system combining Multi-WAN (pfSense) and application-layer load balancing (HAProxy). The system distributes traffic across multiple ISPs using ECMP and balances HTTP requests across multiple web servers with health checks and sticky sessions. Database availability is ensured through MySQL Master-Slave replication.
details: Configured Multi-WAN Load Balancing using Gateway Groups and ECMP
Implemented Failover mechanism to ensure continuous connectivity
Deployed HAProxy as reverse proxy (Layer 7 Load Balancing)
Applied Round Robin and Health Check for backend servers
Implemented Sticky Session for user session persistence
Designed Master-Slave Replication for database redundancy
Segmented network using LAN, DMZ, and WAN architecture
projects.result: Achieved improved system availability, automatic failover between ISPs, balanced traffic across web servers, and ensured database reliability under simulated real-world conditions.

projects.name: SOAR System using Wazuh + AWS
projects.problem: Detect and mitigate brute force SSH attacks
projects.description: Built a SIEM system using Wazuh and integrated AWS Lambda for automated response to security events
projects.result: Automatically blocks attacker IP within seconds

projects.name: Shoe Sales Android Application
projects.problem: Create a convenient and interactive online shopping experience for footwear.
projects.description: Developed a comprehensive mobile e-commerce application featuring user authentication, product filtering, cart management, and order processing. Built the backend API to serve dynamic product data and manage user sessions.
projects.result: Successfully delivered a functional mobile application with a responsive UI, smooth data processing, and reliable backend database integration.

projects.name: Network Monitoring System using Icinga + Grafana
projects.problem: Lack of real-time visibility and alerting in network infrastructure leads to delayed incident detection and system downtime.
projects.description: Designed and deployed a comprehensive network monitoring system using Icinga 2 for infrastructure monitoring, MySQL for data storage, and Grafana for visualization. The system monitors network devices (router, switch, firewall, servers), tracks performance metrics (CPU, memory, bandwidth), and sends real-time alerts via email.
details: Designed monitoring architecture with Icinga Core, database, and visualization layer
Configured host and service monitoring (CPU, RAM, network bandwidth, interface status)
Integrated MySQL for storing monitoring data
Built dashboards in Grafana for real-time visualization
Configured alerting system with email notifications
Used plugins and custom commands to monitor network devices
Simulated real network environment with multiple devices
projects.result: Enabled real-time monitoring and alerting, improved system visibility, reduced response time to incidents, and provided centralized dashboard for network performance analysis.

projects.name: Metasploit SQLi Scanner Module Development
projects.problem: Need a reusable way to detect SQL Injection vulnerabilities in web applications during penetration testing and lab-based security validation.
projects.description: Researched the Metasploit module architecture and developed a custom SQLi auxiliary scanner module. The project included building a controlled Docker-based testing environment with bWAPP, analyzing SQL Injection techniques, and validating the scanner through multiple lab scenarios.
details: Analyzed the internal structure and workflow of Metasploit modules
Built a custom auxiliary scanner module for SQLi detection
Set up a repeatable lab environment using Docker and bWAPP
Evaluated SQLi cases such as boolean-based, union-based, OOB, and file-write scenarios in a controlled environment
Compared the approach with sqlmap to assess automation and effectiveness
projects.result: Produced a reusable SQLi scanning module and a reproducible test environment for security research and penetration-testing practice.

built: High Availability Web Architecture with Failover & Replication
experience.summary: Hands-on experience building and testing real-world security systems on AWS and designing high-availability network architectures with load balancing and failover mechanisms.
